design_philosophy.core_loop: Maneuver fighter craft vertically through eight stages, destroy enemies, collect power-ups and bombs, avoid enemy fire while managing weapon upgrades and special attacks
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precision dodging vs. sustained offensive pressure; managing limited lives and bomb stock against overwhelming waves of enemies
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot an advanced fighter defending Earth from alien invasion using state-of-the-art Crystal technology weapons
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Cooperative synergy mechanic where players can generate special projectiles by shooting each other's craft; rewards coordinated positioning. Design born from financial constraint (cheaper hardware) but elevated through arcade sensibility and borrowing from proven shmup formulas (1942, Xevious, Twin Cobra). Emphasizes accessibility through weapon variety and continues system rather than extreme execution demands

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives per credit; death results in fighter shrapnel becoming damaging projectiles to enemies
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system allows restart at last checkpoint upon credit insertion in arcade; continues system extends to home versions
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; upon continue, player restarts at last stage reached
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Eight stages of increasing difficulty with looping (returns to stage 1 with increased difficulty after stage 8). Player agency through weapon selection (bombs, power-ups) and collectible management. Cooperative play modifies difficulty dynamics through shared pressure and synergy mechanics

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon power-ups and bombs are collectible items that enhance firepower and provide area denial/enemy fire cancellation. Power-up items released when player dies (via fairy mechanic)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression through linear completion; difficulty increases unlock after full cycle completion
replayability_hooks: Cooperative play mechanics reward coordinated strategies
Looping difficulty structure encourages score chasing
Collectible medal items for score increases
Miclus and fairy easter eggs reward exploration of item mechanics

world_building_techniques: Alien Crystals as invasion threat provides external conflict framing
Vanquish Crystal Defense organization contextualizes player role
Fighting Thunder aircraft naming and Crystal technology base establishes narrative justification for mechanics

faction_indicators: Vanquish Crystal Defense (VCD) - human military organization
danger_signals: Military hardware compromised and controlled by invaders
Existential threat requiring humanity's last-resort weapon (Fighting Thunder)
environmental_storytelling: Progression through military/industrial zones implied by stage structure
Crystal corruption of human technology visible through enemy designs
Incremental difficulty representing deeper alien strongholds or reinforcements

sources_used: wikipedia - Raiden (video game) - Gameplay section, Plot section, Development section
